summary: Iteration 10 Testing COMPLETED: Hero Slides CRUD feature fully tested. 13/13 backend API tests passed. All frontend features verified via Playwright UI testing.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/admin/hero-slides returns list of slides (sorted by date_start)
Backend: POST /api/admin/hero-slides creates slide with all fields (title, subtitle, description, dates, slide_type, button, effects, coordinates, revolution params)
Backend: GET /api/admin/hero-slides/{id} returns single slide
Backend: PUT /api/admin/hero-slides/{id} updates slide fields
Backend: DELETE /api/admin/hero-slides/{id} deletes slide
Backend: GET /api/public/hero-slides returns slides for public frontend
Backend: Admin endpoints require authentication (401/403 without token)
Backend: Public endpoint accessible without auth
Backend: 404 returned for nonexistent slide operations
Frontend: /admin/hero shows Hero Slides list table with columns (Title, Subtitle, Type, Start Date, End Date, Actions)
Frontend: /admin/hero has 'Add Slide' button that navigates to /admin/hero/add
Frontend: /admin/hero/add shows complete form with all sections (Timer, Text Content WYSIWYG, Links, Slide Type radio, Background, Animation Effects, Coordinates, Revolution Params)
Frontend: /admin/hero/edit/{id} loads pre-populated form data for editing
Frontend: Delete button shows confirmation dialog with Cancel and Delete buttons
Frontend: Create slide redirects to list with success toast
Frontend: Update slide works and persists changes
Frontend: Delete slide removes from list with success toast
Frontend: Homepage hero section renders slides from hero_slides collection
Frontend: Homepage hero section shows slide indicator dots when multiple slides exist
Frontend: Hero section displays title, subtitle, description with animations
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted test slide 'TEST Second Slide Title' during testing to verify multi-slide functionality
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hero Slides CRUD feature fully implemented and tested. Key components: (1) Backend routes in /app/backend/routes/admin_content.py (lines 31-69) for admin CRUD and /app/backend/routes/public.py (lines 20-25) for public endpoint. (2) Frontend HeroManager.js shows list table with edit/delete actions. (3) HeroSlideForm.js has complete form with Timer, WYSIWYG editors (RichTextEditor), Links, Slide Type (photo/video), Background ImageUpload, Animation Effects dropdowns, X/Y Coordinates, Revolution Slider Parameters. (4) HomePage.js HeroSection component renders slides with animations and indicator dots. (5) Routes configured in App.js: /admin/hero, /admin/hero/add, /admin/hero/edit/:id. (6) API methods in api.js: adminAPI.getHeroSlides/getHeroSlide/createHeroSlide/updateHeroSlide/deleteHeroSlide and publicAPI.getHeroSlides.

backend_api: GET /api/admin/hero-slides - List all slides (sorted by date_start)
POST /api/admin/hero-slides - Create slide with full field support
admin_list_view: Table with columns: Title, Subtitle, Type, Start Date, End Date, Actions
Slide type shown with icon (Photo/Video badge)
admin_form: Timer section: Date Start, Date End (datetime-local inputs)
Text Content section: Title, Subtitle, Description (WYSIWYG RichTextEditor)
Links section: Button Text, Button URL, Window Open (same/new)
Slide Type section: Photo/Video radio buttons
Photo/Video upload: ImageUpload component or video embed textarea
Animation Effects: 5 dropdowns (title, subtitle, description, button, media) with top/right/bottom/left options
Revolution Slider: Transition, Slot Amount, Master Speed, Delay, Speed/Layer
Data Start per Layer: Title/Subtitle/Description/Button/Media start times (ms)
Create/Update Slide button at top and bottom
homepage_hero: Falls back to legacy single-doc hero when no slides
Shows slide indicator dots for multiple slides
Displays title, subtitle, description with animations
Supports photo and video slide types
